title: MetricAnchor Semantic Model
description: Defines business metrics, dimensions, entities, synonyms, and rules for a dataset.

name.description: Unique identifier for this semantic model. Lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores only.

dataset.description: Name of the registered dataset this model applies to.

description.description: Human-readable description of this model and its intended use.

grain.description: What one row represents. Plain English. E.g. 'one row = one completed order'.

time_column.description: Primary date/timestamp column for time-based filtering and trend questions.

entities.description: Key business objects in this dataset (customers, orders, tickets, users).

column.description: Column that uniquely identifies this entity (usually an ID column).

metrics.description: Business measures — numeric values computed by aggregating the data.

name.description: Unique metric name used in natural language questions.

description.description: Plain-English definition of this metric. Shown verbatim in trust output.

expression.description: SQL aggregate expression. E.g. SUM(order_total), COUNT(DISTINCT user_id), AVG(resolution_time_hours).

value.description: Scalar string/number, or array for IN / NOT IN operators.

aliases.description: Alternative names users might use when asking about this metric.

default_aggregation.description: How to aggregate this metric when no explicit aggregation is requested.

dimensions.description: Columns users can group by or filter on in natural language.

column.description: Actual column name in the dataset.

values.description: Known categorical values for this dimension. Helps the LLM avoid hallucinating values.

is_date.description: Set true if this dimension is a date or timestamp column.

synonyms.description: Map natural language phrases to model concepts. Helps users refer to metrics/dimensions by colloquial names.

phrase.description: The phrase a user might type, e.g. 'sales', 'customers', 'churned accounts'.

maps_to.description: What this phrase maps to. Format: metric:<name>, dimension:<name>, or entity:<name>.

business_rules.description: Always-applied SQL filters that encode domain logic not obvious from raw data.

description.description: Why this rule exists and what it excludes.

filter.description: SQL WHERE clause fragment. Applied globally unless a metric/dimension overrides it.

applies_to.description: Restrict this rule to specific metrics. Omit to apply globally.

caveats.description: Plain-English warnings shown in the trust panel for every answer from this model.

exclusions.description: Explicit data exclusions — what is intentionally NOT counted, and why.

description.description: Plain-English description of what is excluded.

filter.description: The SQL condition that implements this exclusion.

relationships.description: Describes how this dataset relates to others (for future join support).
